body.text: The most fertile and most widely found माती in India. Formed by rivers depositing silt over thousands of years. Found along Ganga, Yamuna, Brahmaputra and other river plains.
Punjab, Haryana, UP, Bihar, West Bengal, Assam river plains. Also coastal areas of Odisha, Tamil Nadu, Kerala.

items: Rich in potash, phosphoric acid and lime
Light and easy to plow
High पाणी-holding capacity
Well-drained — no पाणीlogging
pH: 6.5–7.5 (near neutral — good for most पीकs)
✅ Wheat
✅ Rice
✅ Sugarcane
✅ Maize
✅ Oilseeds
✅ Pulses
✅ Vegetables
Plow 3–4 times before sowing for fine seedbed
Add organic manure (compost/FYM) regularly — गाळाची loses organic matter fast
Follow पीक rotation to maintain fertility
Apply nitrogen fertilizer in split doses
Don't leave the field without पीक — topमाती erodes fast
Don't over-irrigate — पाणीlogging is the biggest risk
Don't skip potash fertilizer — this माती often lacks K

body.text: गाळाची माती covers about 43% of India's total land area and feeds over 60% of the country's population.
Also called 'Regur' or 'cotton माती'. Formed from volcanic lava rocks millions of years ago. Dark black color because of iron, magnesium, and organic matter.
Maharashtra, Madhya Pradesh, Gujarat, Andhra Pradesh, Karnataka, Telangana — the Deccan Plateau region.
Black माती swells when wet and cracks when dry. These cracks aerate the माती from inside — nature's own plowing. You'll see 5–15 cm wide cracks in summer.

items: Excellent पाणी-holding capacity — holds moisture for long
Rich in calcium, magnesium, potash and lime
High clay content — sticky when wet
pH: 7.5–8.5 (slightly alkaline)
✅ Cotton (best!)
✅ Soybean
✅ Sorghum (Jowar)
✅ Wheat
✅ Sunflower
✅ Citrus fruits
Plow before rains — माती is very hard when dry
Mix organic matter regularly — this माती lacks nitrogen and phosphorus
Use raised beds for पाणीlogging-prone areas
Add zinc and sulfur — black माती is often deficient in micronutrients
Don't try to plow when माती is wet — extremely sticky, damages tools
Don't पाणीlog — drainage is poor, roots will rot
Don't skip interपीकping — single पीक depletes nitrogen fast

body.text: Black माती is so rich in calcium that lime application is rarely needed. The माती itself acts as a buffer.
Red color comes from iron oxide (rust). Formed from हवामानing of old crystalline rocks. Porous and well-drained but loses पाणी fast.
Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, Odisha, Jharkhand, Chhattisgarh, parts of Karnataka and Maharashtra.

items: Low nitrogen, phosphorus and humus — needs fertilizer
Rich in iron and potassium
Sandy texture — पाणी drains fast
Responds very well to manure and fertilizers
pH: 5.5–7 (slightly acidic)
✅ Groundnut
✅ Millets (Bajra, Ragi)
✅ Pulses
✅ Potato
✅ Fruits (mango, banana)
✅ Tobacco
Add heavy doses of FYM and compost before sowing
Apply lime if माती is acidic (below pH 6)
Irrigate frequently but in small amounts
Mulching is very beneficial — prevents moisture loss
Don't rely only on chemical fertilizer — manure is essential
Don't leave माती bare — apply mulch or grow cover पीकs
Don't grow heavy पाणी पीकs like paddy without पाणी availability

body.text: Red माती turns yellow when it is found in lower areas or पाणीlogged zones, because iron is in a different chemical state there.
Formed in heavy-rainfall tropical areas where nutrients are washed deep into the ground by rain (leaching). Hard like brick when dry (the word 'later' means brick in Latin).
Kerala, Karnataka, parts of Assam, Odisha, Meghalaya. Western Ghats and Eastern Ghats highland areas.

items: Very acidic — pH 4.5 to 6
Poor in nitrogen, phosphorus, calcium, magnesium
Rich in iron and aluminium (reason for red-brown color)
Hardens in dry हवामान — difficult to plow
✅ Tea
✅ Coffee
✅ Cashew
✅ Coconut
✅ Rubber
✅ Tapioca
Apply heavy lime before any पीक — acidity must be corrected first
Add large amounts of compost and green manure
Plow immediately after rains when माती is soft
Mulching is essential to prevent moisture loss
Don't grow wheat or rice without major माती treatment first
Don't delay lime application — acidic माती kills most पीकs
Don't leave माती bare — will harden into brick-like surface

body.text: Tea and coffee thrive in laterite because they naturally love acidic माती. This is the main reason these पीकs are grown in the Western Ghats.
Large sand particles with almost no clay or organic matter. पाणी passes through immediately. Very hot in summer, cold in winter.
Rajasthan, parts of Gujarat, Punjab and Haryana near Thar Desert.

items: Extremely low पाणी retention
Low in all nutrients — nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium
High in salt in some areas
pH: 7 to 8.5 (alkaline)
Very high temperature variation — hot days, cold nights
✅ Bajra (Pearl Millet)
✅ Moth Bean
✅ Guar (Cluster Bean)
✅ Sesame
✅ Date Palm
✅ Pomegranate
Add organic matter every season — compost, FYM, green manure
Drip irrigation is best — reduces पाणी waste by 60%
Windbreaks (tree rows) reduce wind erosion significantly
Apply gypsum if salt is high
Don't grow paddy, sugarcane — needs far too much पाणी
Don't over-irrigate — causes salt buildup (salinization)
Don't plow too deep — brings up infertile subमाती

body.text: Sandy माती, despite being very poor for farming, is ideal for growing drought-tolerant पीकs like Bajra which can produce good yields with just 200–350mm of rain.
Plants need 17 nutrients total. Nitrogen (N), Phosphorus (P), and Potassium (K) are needed in the largest amounts. Called 'primary macronutrients'.
Makes leaves dark green and plants grow fast. Part of chlorophyll — what gives leaves their green color. Gets used up fastest in माती.

items: Deficiency signs: Yellowing of older/lower leaves first. Plant looks pale. Slow growth.
Sources: Urea (46% N), CAN, Ammonium sulfate, cow dung, compost, green manure
Memory trick: N = Neem patta (green leaf)

body.text: Essential for root development, flower formation and fruit ripening. Helps in energy transfer inside plant cells.

items: Deficiency signs: Purple or red-brown color on underside of leaves. Weak roots. Delayed maturity.
Sources: DAP (18% N + 46% P), SSP (16% P), bone meal, rock phosphate
Memory trick: P = Phool-Phal (flowers and fruits)

body.text: Makes plants strong and disease-resistant. Helps regulate पाणी use. Improves quality of fruits, vegetables and grains.

items: Deficiency signs: Brown, scorched edges on older leaves. Weak stems. Poor grain quality.
Sources: MOP (60% K), SOP (50% K), wood ash, banana peels, compost
Memory trick: K = Kaath (strong wood)

body.text: Always apply phosphorus and potassium at sowing time. Apply nitrogen in splits — half at sowing, rest after 30 days. This way plants get nutrients when they need them most.
Needed in tiny amounts but deficiency causes serious पीक loss. Zinc is the most common deficiency in Indian मातीs.

items: Deficiency: White or light green color between leaf veins on young leaves. Called 'khaira disease' in rice.
Fix: Zinc sulfate (ZnSO4) — 25 kg/acre as माती application, or 0.5% foliar spray
Prevention: Apply 10 kg zinc sulfate every 3 years
Deficiency: Yellow leaves but veins remain green (opposite of zinc). Common in alkaline मातीs.
Fix: Ferrous sulfate (FeSO4) 0.5% foliar spray. Most effective early morning.
Note: Common in calcareous/alkaline मातीs of Punjab, Haryana
Deficiency: Death of growing tip. Hollow stems. Poor fruit set.
Fix: Borax 1 kg/acre माती application or 0.2% foliar spray
Critical in: Cauliflower, sunflower, groundnut
Deficiency: Yellowing of young leaves. Stunted growth.
Fix: Gypsum (calcium sulfate) 100–200 kg/acre. Also corrects alkalinity.
पीकs that need most sulfur: Mustard, onion, garlic, pulses

body.text: माती testing reveals which micronutrients your माती lacks. Get a free माती Health Card test from your local agriculture department. A test every 2–3 years saves you money on unnecessary fertilizers.
Organic fertilizers release nutrients slowly, improve माती structure, and support beneficial microbes. They make chemical fertilizers work better too.

items: Composition: 0.5% N, 0.25% P, 0.5% K on average
Use: 4–5 tons/acre 3–4 weeks before sowing (so it rots fully)
Make: Compost cow dung + straw + urine for 3–4 months
Value: ₹500–800 per ton
5x more nutrients than regular FYM
Composition: 1.5% N, 1% P, 0.5% K + many micronutrients
Use: 2–3 tons/acre
Sell extra: ₹5–8 per kg — good side income
Grow Sesbania (Dhaincha) or Sunhemp for 45–55 days, then plow into field
Adds 40–60 kg Nitrogen per acre — equal to 90–130 kg Urea!
Best practice: Grow between two main पीकs

body.text: Mix in 200-liter drum: 10 kg fresh cow dung + 10 liters cow urine + 2 kg jaggery + 2 kg besan (gram flour) + handful of माती from banyan tree base. Stir twice daily for 48 hours. Dilute 1:10 and apply in irrigation पाणी or spray.
Using 50% chemical fertilizer + 50% organic sources gives equal or better yield than 100% chemical, at 30–40% lower cost.
India's most important rabi (winter) पीक. Sown after kharif harvest. Needs cool winters and warm summers. Over 30 million hectares grown annually.

items: Field preparation: October–November
Sowing: 1–25 November (best window)
First irrigation: 20–21 days (crown root stage)
Harvest: March–April
Best माती: गाळाची/loamy माती, good drainage
Plow 3–4 times, last pass with rotavator for fine seedbed
Add 4–5 tons FYM before last plowing
Target माती pH: 6–7.5
Seed rate: 40–45 kg/acre (timely sowing), 50–55 kg/acre (late sowing)
Depth: 5–6 cm
Row spacing: 20–22 cm
Varieties: HD-2967, GW-322, PBW-343, HD-3086
DAP: 50 kg/acre at sowing
Urea: Split in 3 doses — 1/3 at sowing, 1/3 at 1st irrigation, 1/3 at 2nd irrigation
MOP: 20 kg/acre at sowing
Zinc sulfate: 10 kg/acre if zinc deficient
1st: 20–21 days — Crown Root Initiation (MUST, most critical)
2nd: 40–45 days — Tillering
3rd: 60–65 days — Jointing
4th: 80–85 days — Flowering/Heading (MUST)
5th: 100–105 days — Milk/Dough stage
6th: 115–120 days — Grain filling (optional)
Treat seed with Carboxin + Thiram before sowing (prevents loose smut)
Sow on time — each week's delay after Nov 25 reduces yield by 1–1.5 quintal/acre
Apply Urea in splits — improves nitrogen use efficiency by 30%
Don't sow after December 15 — 'late sown' varieties are different
Don't burn stubble — adds carbon, prevents माती erosion, retains moisture
Don't over-irrigate at 1st irrigation — light irrigation only, else 'lodging' occurs

body.text: Wheat sown on November 1–10 consistently gives 2–4 quintals/acre more than wheat sown on December 1–10, all else being equal.
India's most important kharif (summer-monsoon) पीक. Feeds more than half of India. Requires flooded conditions in most varieties.

items: Nursery preparation: May–June
Transplanting: June–July (after 25–30 days in nursery)
Panicle initiation: August–September
Harvest: October–November
Area: 100 sq meters per acre of main field
Seed rate: 20–25 kg/acre (for transplanting)
Seed treatment: Soak 24 hours, then incubate 24 hours for germination
Treat with Carbendazim 2g/kg seed
Age of seedling: 25–30 days (not older — older seedlings give less yield)
Row × plant spacing: 20 × 15 cm
Transplant 2–3 seedlings per hill
Best time: early morning or evening — not in peak heat
Nitrogen: 50–60 kg/acre total, split in 3 doses
Dose 1 (Basal, at transplanting): 1/3 Urea
Dose 2 (Tillering, 20 days after transplant): 1/3 Urea
Dose 3 (Panicle initiation, 50 days): 1/3 Urea
Phosphorus (DAP): 25 kg/acre at transplanting only
0–7 days after transplant: 3–5 cm standing पाणी
7–50 days: Maintain 5 cm standing पाणी
50–65 days: Drain field once for 5–7 days (increases yield!)
65 days–harvest: Maintain 3–5 cm पाणी
10 days before harvest: Drain completely
Use SRI method (System of Rice Intensification) — increases yield by 20–30% with 25% less पाणी
Apply zinc sulfate 10 kg/acre (paddy मातीs are most zinc-deficient)
Scout for Brown Plant Hopper weekly — most damaging pest
Don't transplant seedlings older than 35 days — yield drops sharply
Don't apply urea when पाणी is standing deep — it escapes as gas
Don't delay harvest after maturity — grain shattering loss

body.text: The mid-season drainage (draining the field for 5–7 days around 50 days after transplanting) increases yield by 10–15% by forcing roots deeper and reducing unproductive tillers.
India's 'white gold'. Major cash पीक. Needs black माती and warm climate. Modern Bt cotton (hybrid) gives 3–4x higher yield than older varieties.

items: Sowing: May–June (after summer rains begin)
Vegetative stage: July–August
Boll development: September–October
Picking (3–4 pickings): October–December
Best माती: Deep black माती (regur), good moisture retention
Deep plowing (30 cm) in summer before sowing
Ridge and furrow method preferred — better drainage
Spacing: 90 × 60 cm (hybrid varieties), 60 × 30 cm (desi)
Nitrogen: 40–50 kg/acre (apply in 3 splits to avoid vegetative excess)
Phosphorus: 20–25 kg/acre at sowing
Potassium: 20 kg/acre at sowing
Sulphur: 10 kg/acre (very important for cotton)
Use only certified Bt hybrid seeds from reliable source
Grow refugia (non-Bt rows) as per guidelines — 5% of area
Spray for bollworm at first sign — losses are massive if delayed
Multiple pickings — do not wait for all bolls to open
Don't over-apply nitrogen — causes excess leaf growth, reduces fruiting
Don't पाणीlog — cotton roots cannot tolerate standing पाणी
Don't use same pesticide repeatedly — pests develop resistance fast

body.text: American Bollworm, Pink Bollworm, and Tobacco Streak Virus are the three biggest threats to cotton in India. Weekly scouting from July onwards saves 30–50% of yield.
Pulses (lentils, chickpea, moong, urad, arhar) are nature's fertilizer factories. Their roots carry bacteria that fix atmospheric nitrogen into माती — free fertilizer!

A special bacteria called Rhizobium lives in the roots of pulses. It converts nitrogen gas from air (78% of air is N2) into a form plants and माती can use. After harvesting, this nitrogen stays in the माती for the next पीक.

items: Buy Rhizobium culture from agriculture shop (₹30–50 per pack)
Mix with jaggery solution and coat seeds before sowing
Increases yield by 10–20% and adds 20–30 kg N per acre to माती
Critical for first-time pulse cultivation in a field
Chickpea (Chana) — Rabi, Oct–Mar, best in गाळाची/black माती
Lentil (Masoor) — Rabi, Oct–Mar, cool and dry climate
Moong (Green Gram) — Zaid & Kharif, fastest growing (60–70 days)
Urad (Black Gram) — Kharif, hot and humid, south India
Arhar/Toor — Kharif, 150–180 days, needs deep black माती
Always inoculate seed with Rhizobium before sowing
Grow pulses before wheat in rotation — wheat gets free nitrogen bonus
Apply only phosphorus fertilizer at sowing (no N needed!)
Thin sowing is better — overcrowding reduces pod formation
Don't apply nitrogen fertilizer — it actually reduces fixation by bacteria
Don't पाणीlog — all pulses have very low flood tolerance
Don't spray insecticide when flowers are open — kills pollinator bees

body.text: Growing pulses in rotation is equivalent to applying 30–60 kg of Urea per acre for free. This is called 'biological nitrogen fixation' and reduces fertilizer cost significantly.
How you पाणी your पीकs affects both yield and profit. Modern irrigation methods save पाणी and reduce cost — but need initial investment.

items: Cost: ₹0 setup
पाणी saved: 0% (most wasteful)
Suitable for: Paddy, sugarcane
Disadvantage: 40–60% पाणी wasted by evaporation and runoff
Labor: High — need to watch and manage पाणी flow
Cost: Very low (just tractor for making furrows)
पाणी saved: 20–30% vs flood
Suitable for: Vegetables, maize, sugarcane, cotton
Method: पाणी flows through furrows between rows, not over entire field
Cost: ₹40,000–60,000 per acre (80–90% subsidy for small farmers!)
पाणी saved: 50–70% vs flood
Suitable for: Fruits, vegetables, banana, pomegranate, cotton, sugarcane
Also delivers fertilizers through pipes — called 'Fertigation'
Government subsidy: 90% for SC/ST, 75% for small farmers under PMKSY
Cost: ₹15,000–25,000 per acre (subsidy available)
पाणी saved: 30–50% vs flood
Suitable for: Wheat, vegetables, groundnut, fodder पीकs
Best for sandy/light मातीs where पाणी needs to be applied gently
Cost: ₹0 extra
पाणी saved: 25–50% vs traditional paddy flooding
Yield increase: 20–50% over conventional transplanting
Method: Young seedlings (8–12 days), wider spacing, moist but not flooded

body.text: With government subsidies under PMKSY (PM Krishi Sinchayee Yojana), a small farmer can install drip irrigation at just ₹4,000–8,000 per acre. Apply at district agriculture office.
पाणीing at wrong time wastes पाणी and reduces yield. Every पीक has 'critical stages' when पाणी stress causes permanent yield loss.
Simple Field Test for Irrigation Need
Push a wooden stick 6 inches into माती. Pull out. If moist माती is stuck to stick — no irrigation needed. If stick comes out dry and clean — irrigate today.

items: Wheat: Crown root (21 days) ★★★, Tillering (45 days) ★★★, Flowering (80–90 days) ★★★
Rice: Transplanting + Tillering + Flowering + Grain filling (all critical)
Maize: Tasseling + Silking stage (55–65 days) — MOST critical. Skip = 50% yield loss
Cotton: Boll formation (60–90 days after sowing) — most critical
Groundnut: Pegging stage (35–45 days) and Pod filling (90–105 days)
Soybean: Flowering to pod filling (50–80 days)
Leaves curl inward (plant reducing surface area to save पाणी)
Wilting in morning hours (severe stress)
Blue-green color change in leaves
Premature leaf drop
Yellow lower leaves (nitrogen being washed down)
Stunted growth despite irrigation
Root rot (foul smell from माती)
White salt crust on माती surface

body.text: पाणीing in the evening is better than afternoon — reduces evaporation by 20–30%. Early morning is best for sprinkler as leaves dry quickly and prevent disease.
Early identification saves 50–80% of पीक loss. Scout your field every week — walk in a 'W' pattern covering all corners. Look at 20 random plants each time.

items: Signs: Tiny green/black/brown insects clustering on leaf undersides and tender shoots. Sticky honeydew substance. Curled leaves.
Most active: Cool हवामान (Nov–Feb)
Fix: Imidacloprid 17.8 SL — 0.5 ml per liter पाणी. Or Neem oil 5 ml/liter.
Natural enemy: Ladybird beetles eat aphids — don't spray if ladybirds are present
Signs: Tiny white flying insects. Yellow leaves. Spreads deadly viruses like TYLCV in tomato, CLCuD in cotton.
Fix: Yellow sticky traps (₹5 each) + Thiamethoxam 25 WG 0.3g/liter
Prevention: Mineral oil spray (5 ml/liter) prevents virus transmission
Signs: In young plants: 'Dead heart' — central leaf turns yellow and dies. In older plants: 'White ear' — panicle is empty.
Affects: Rice, maize, sugarcane, sorghum
Fix: Release Trichogramma egg parasitoid (biological control) or Chlorpyriphos 10G granules in plant whorl
Signs: Silver-grey streaks on leaves. Curled leaf margins. Tiny thread-like insects visible with hand lens.
Affects: Onion, cotton, chili, groundnut
Fix: Spinosad 45% SC — 0.3 ml/liter or Fipronil 5% SC — 1.5 ml/liter
Signs: 'Hopperburn' — circular patches of dried, brown rice plants. Insects at base of plant.
Very destructive — can destroy entire field in 7–10 days
Fix: Drain पाणी from field + Buprofezin 25 SC spray. Do NOT spray Pyrethroids — causes outbreak!

body.text: Setting up light traps attracts and kills moths at night — reduces egg-laying. One light trap per 2 acres saves one pesticide spray worth ₹500–800 per acre.
Most पीक diseases are caused by fungi (70%), bacteria (20%), or viruses (10%). Fungal diseases can be treated with fungicides. Viral diseases have no cure — prevention only.
Yellow Rust / Stripe Rust (Pila Ttika) — Wheat

items: Signs: Yellow-orange powdery stripes running along wheat leaves. In rows — not random patches.
Favors: Cool हवामान (10–15°C) with light rain or dew. Feb–March window.
Fix: Propiconazole (Tilt) 25 EC — 1 ml/liter. Spray immediately, don't wait. Repeat after 14 days if needed.
Economic threshold: Spray when 5% plants show infection
Signs: Grey-centered, brown-bordered diamond shaped spots on leaves. Most dangerous: 'Neck blast' — panicle base turns grey and breaks.
Favors: High humidity + low temperature at night + excess nitrogen.
Fix: Tricyclazole 75% WP — 0.6g/liter or Isoprothiolane 40% EC — 1.5 ml/liter
Prevention: Do NOT apply excess nitrogen. Use blast-resistant varieties.
Signs: White powdery coating on leaves and stems. Looks like flour dusted on plant.
Affects: Wheat, peas, cucurbits, grapes, mango
Favors: Cool, cloudy हवामान. High humidity.
Fix: Sulfur 80% WP — 2g/liter. Or Hexaconazole 5% EC — 2 ml/liter. Or Carbendazim 50% WP — 1g/liter
Signs: पाणी-soaked lesions from leaf tip/margins. Becomes yellow-white as it spreads. 'Kresek' — seedlings wilt completely.
Favors: Flooding after transplanting, high nitrogen, typhoon/storm damage
Fix: NO effective chemical cure for bacterial diseases. Copper oxychloride gives partial control. Drain field.
Prevention: Resistant varieties, avoid excess N, clean seeds
Signs: Plant suddenly wilts and dies even though माती has पाणी. Vascular (पाणी-conducting) tissue turns brown inside stem.
Affects: Tomato, cotton, chickpea, banana
Cause: Fusarium or Verticillium fungi in माती
Fix: No cure for individual plant. Remove and burn. Drench माती with Carbendazim + Copper mixture.
Prevention: पीक rotation, resistant varieties, healthy seedlings from disease-free nursery

body.text: Most fungal diseases spread in spores carried by wind. A single infected plant produces millions of spores. Scout and remove infected plants immediately — this 'roguing' prevents 60–80% of spread.
IPM means using all pest control tools together — cultural, biological, chemical — in the right order. First try non-chemical methods. Use pesticides only as last resort.

items: Deep summer plowing — kills pupae and egg masses in माती
Seed treatment — kills seed-borne diseases before they start
पीक rotation — breaks pest life cycle
Trap पीकs — grow marigold border to attract pests away from main पीक
Resistant varieties — biggest impact, zero cost
Trichogramma cards — egg parasitoid wasps, kill moth eggs (₹20–30/acre)
NPV (Nuclear Polyhedrosis Virus) — bio-pesticide for Spodoptera and Heliothis
Pseudomonas fluorescens — spray to prevent माती-borne diseases
Neem products — Neem oil (5 ml/L) or NSKE 5% — disrupts pest feeding and breeding
Apply only when pest population crosses 'Economic Threshold Level' (ETL)
Use recommended doses — more is NOT better
Rotate chemical groups to prevent resistance
Don't spray within 10 days of harvest (pre-harvest interval)
Use proper PPE — mask, gloves, boots while spraying
Read label fully before use
Wear protective gear — never spray without it
Never spray against wind direction
Wash hands, face, clothes after spraying
Store pesticides in original container, locked, away from children
Never eat, drink, smoke while handling pesticides

body.text: Studies show that IPM reduces pesticide use by 50–60% while maintaining the same yield. It saves ₹1,500–3,000 per acre in pesticide costs alone.
Organic farming uses no synthetic fertilizers or pesticides. Takes 3 years to certify. Premium price is 20–50% higher than regular market. Both profitability and माती health improve long-term.

items: Year 1: Stop all chemical inputs. Add maximum organic matter. Yield may drop 15–30%.
Year 2: Yields recover. माती biology rebuilds. Apply Jeevamrit, vermicompost regularly.
Year 3: Yields match or exceed conventional. Apply for PGS/organic certification.
After Year 3: Certified organic, sell at premium market
PGS = Participatory Guarantee System (government run, free)
Form a group of 5–50 farmers in same village
Register online at pgsindia-ncof.gov.in
Annual inspection by group members + local officer
Get PGS Organic certificate + green logo for premium sale
Direct sale to city consumers (WhatsApp groups, residential colonies)
Organic markets in cities — Delhi Organic Haat, etc.
E-commerce: BigBasket, Amazon, Nature's Basket
Restaurants, hotels that want certified organic supply
Export — highest premium, needs NPOP certification
Start with 1 acre to learn before converting whole farm
Maintain detailed diary — required for certification
Develop local market first before converting more land
Join organic farmer group — shared knowledge and costs

body.text: A group of farmers in Maharashtra converted to organic and started selling directly to Mumbai consumers via WhatsApp group. They get ₹40/kg for organic wheat vs ₹22/kg market rate — 82% premium.

articles.title: All Farmer Schemes in One Place

body.text: The government has many schemes that directly help farmers. Most are underutilized because farmers don't know about them. Here's a complete guide.

items: Amount: ₹6,000/year in 3 installments of ₹2,000 each
Who qualifies: Small/marginal farmers with land in own name
How to apply: pmkisan.gov.in OR visit nearest Common Service Centre (CSC)
Documents: Aadhaar, land records (Khatoni), bank account linked to Aadhaar
You pay only: 1.5% of sum insured (Rabi), 2% (Kharif), 5% (horticulture)
Government pays remaining 90–95%!
Covers: Drought, flood, hailstorm, pest attack, post-harvest losses
How to apply: Through bank before sowing. Or KCC loan auto-enrolls you.
Claim: Report पीक damage within 72 hours (call 14447)
Subsidy on drip irrigation: 90% for small/marginal, 75% for others
Covers: Drip, sprinkler, rain-gun systems
How to apply: District Agriculture Office / State Horticulture Department
Also covers: पाणी storage structures, farm ponds
Interest rate: Only 4% per year (vs 24–36% moneylender rate)
Loan amount: Up to ₹3 lakhs (higher for bigger farms)
Use for: Seeds, fertilizers, labor, equipment, post-harvest expenses
How to apply: Any nationalized bank or cooperative bank with land documents

body.text: माती Health Card — Free माती Test

items: What you get: Exact NPK levels, pH, micronutrients of your माती
How often: Free testing every 2 years
What to do: Apply recommended fertilizers from card — saves 15–20% fertilizer cost
Where: District agriculture department or Krishi Vigyan Kendra (KVK)
What: Online national market for selling पीकs directly across India
Website: enam.gov.in
Benefit: Get competitive bids from buyers across the country
Requirement: Register at nearest linked mandi (APMC)

body.text: Most farmers lose ₹20,000–50,000 per year by not claiming PM-KISAN and पीक insurance they're eligible for. Check with your local agriculture officer to verify your eligibility.
